statement.markdown: Write a recursive function `binarySearch` that takes two arguments `arr` and `target` and returns the index of the target element if found. The function should return -1 if the target element is not found. The function should also handle cases where the input array is empty or not sorted.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be an array of integers and a target integer.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the index of the target element if found, otherwise return -1.
statement.notes: The function should be recursive and should not use any loops.

examples.explanation: The target element 9 is found at index 4 in the array.
The target element 7 is not found in the array.

hints.text: Use a recursive approach to find the target element in the array.
Make sure to handle the base cases where the input array is empty or the target element is not found.
Test your function with different input arrays and target elements to ensure it works correctly.

editorial.title: Fix the Recursive Binary Search
editorial.intuition: The function should use a recursive approach to find the target element in the array. It should divide the array into two halves and search for the target element in one of the halves.
editorial.bruteForce: A simple recursive function that checks each element in the array.
editorial.betterSolution: A recursive function that divides the array into two halves and searches for the target element in one of the halves.
editorial.optimalSolution: A recursive function that uses a binary search approach to find the target element in the array.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function should return the index of the target element if found, otherwise return -1. The function should also handle cases where the input array is empty or not sorted.
commonMistakes: Not handling the base cases correctly.
Not dividing the array into two halves.
Not searching for the target element in one of the halves.
